title: OMCs absorbing ₹550 Crore daily losses from petrol, diesel, LPG amid conflict-led disruptions: Government
content:
Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) are absorbing daily losses amounting to ₹550 Crore.
*   These losses are incurred from the sale of petrol, diesel, and LPG.
*   The government attributes these disruptions and subsequent losses to ongoing global conflicts.

title: Supreme Court sets aside NCLAT order which rejected Amazon's appeal
content:
The Supreme Court has set aside an order previously issued by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT).
*   The NCLAT's original order had rejected an appeal filed by Amazon.
*   This decision by the Supreme Court effectively nullifies the rejection of Amazon's appeal by the NCLAT.

title: “This is so stupid”: Candace Owens challenges Blake Neff over Charlie Kirk and Erika Kirk TPUSA successor claims
content:
Candace Owens publicly challenged Blake Neff, vehemently dismissing recent claims as "stupid."
*   The core of the dispute revolves around speculation concerning who might succeed Charlie Kirk at Turning Point USA (TPUSA).
*   Specifically, the claims involve Charlie Kirk and his wife, Erika Kirk, in discussions about TPUSA's future leadership.

title: Annapurna Yojana: How to enrol in West Bengal’s new cash aid scheme for women
content:
It announces the "Annapurna Yojana," a new cash aid scheme.
*   The scheme is specifically for women in West Bengal.
*   The article will provide instructions on how to enrol in this program.

title: Supreme Court refuses urgent hearing of PIL seeking complete ban on cow slaughter
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The Supreme Court refused to grant an urgent hearing for a case.
*   This case was a Public Interest Litigation (PIL).
*   The PIL sought a complete ban on cow slaughter.

title: Supreme Court to examine if CBSE’s three-language rule is ‘unreasonable’ on kids, resources
content:
The Supreme Court is set to review the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)'s three-language policy.
*   The court will specifically assess whether this rule is 'unreasonable'.
*   Concerns being examined include the policy's potential impact on students and educational resources.

title: Will file curative petition against youngster’s acquittal from 2017 Mugalivakkam child sexual assault-cum-murder case, T.N. govt. tells Madras High Court
content:
The T.N. government has informed the Madras High Court of its intent to file a curative petition.
*   This petition will challenge the acquittal of a youngster.
*   The acquittal was related to the 2017 Mugalivakkam child sexual assault-cum-murder case.

title: Supreme Court upholds SIR exercise as an advancement towards free and fair elections
content:
The Supreme Court has affirmed the validity of the SIR exercise.
*   The court views this exercise as a progressive measure in the electoral process.
*   It is specifically deemed an advancement towards ensuring free and fair elections.
